features.name: AI Documentation Help
features.description: Answers user questions about Datagrok by searching and synthesizing answers from platform documentation and public code via an LLM, surfaced through the platform's Powersearch panel.
features.evidence_summary: Help is an aiSearchProvider described as 'Get answers from AI assistant based on Datagrok documentation'; combinedLLMSearchProvider wires it into the platform search infrastructure; changelog entry 'AI: Powersearch Search AI features' confirms the integration.

features.name: AI Function Execution
features.description: Plans and executes chains of Datagrok functions to achieve a goal described in natural language, enabling agentic task completion within the platform.
features.evidence_summary: Execute is an aiSearchProvider described as 'Plans and executes function steps to achieve needed results'; the 1.0.0 changelog entry 'Invoking function and chains of functions' directly names this feature.

features.name: AI Query Matching
features.description: Matches a natural-language prompt to an existing Datagrok SQL query using fuzzy/pattern search and executes it, backed by tools that index database schemas and persist query metadata.
features.evidence_summary: Query, findMatchingPatternQuery, setupAIQueryEditor, indexDatabaseSchema, and moveMetaToDB share a naming and purpose cluster around AI-driven SQL discovery; changelog entries 'Fuzzy matching' and 'AI sql' trace the feature's origin.

features.name: Plugin Lifecycle and LLM Client Infrastructure
features.description: Handles package initialization, autostart registration, and the underlying LLM client setup (Azure, Gemini/Vercel AI, proxy) that all Grokky AI providers depend on.
features.evidence_summary: init and autostart are standard Datagrok plugin lifecycle hooks; changelog entries for Azure client, Vercel AI refactor, proxy move, and structured-output session reuse are all LLM-client infrastructure changes not tied to a specific user-facing capability.
